name: Draft subscription-cancellation request
summary: Generate a cancellation letter citing the user's click-to-cancel rights in applicable jurisdictions.

evidence.claim: CA SB-313 requires online-signup subscriptions to permit online cancellation; NY, IL, VT have analogous state laws. Federal FTC Click-to-Cancel Rule was vacated July 2025.

body.canonicalName: Draft subscription-cancellation request with jurisdictional citations
body.description: Generate a plain-text or email cancellation request citing the user's click-to-cancel rights (CA SB-313, NY/IL/VT analogs) if applicable. Also generates a dispute-route to the state AG or CFPB if the business refuses compliant cancellation (E1).

prerequisites.description: User has named the service they want to cancel
User's state of residence determines which law to cite

template.subject: Cancellation request — {service_name}

stateLawSnippets.CA: Under California Business & Professions Code §17602 (SB-313), my online-signup subscription is entitled to online cancellation through the same channel I signed up.
stateLawSnippets.NY: Under New York General Business Law §527-a, my online-signup subscription is entitled to online cancellation.
stateLawSnippets.IL: Under the Illinois Automatic Contract Renewal Act, online-signup subscriptions must provide an online cancellation mechanism.
stateLawSnippets.VT: Under Vermont 9 V.S.A. §2454a, online subscription services must provide online cancellation.
stateLawSnippets.DEFAULT: I request cancellation through a simple online mechanism matching the signup channel, consistent with consumer-protection standards emerging in US state law.
